briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech Engineering Handbook](https://w.pitula.me/fintech-engineering-handbook/)** — *Hacker News* — A comprehensive technical resource for engineers building fintech products has gained significant community attention.

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their purported AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are set to transition to a sovereign European payment network, reducing reliance on Visa and Mastercard.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of embedded banking middleware provider Synapse, highlighting risks in the BaaS model.

- **[Centrifuge and New York Life Investment Management Partner to Tokenize U.S. High Yield Corporate Bond Strategy](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-wire-news-releases-pmn/centrifuge-and-new-york-life-investment-management-partner-to-tokenize-u-s-high-yield-corporate-bond-strategy)** — *Financial Post* — Centrifuge and New York Life Investment Management have partnered to bring one of the first high-yield bond offerings on-chain, marking a milestone in institutional asset tokenization.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[RBC fined $4.25M over incorrect credit card statements](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/rbc-fined-credit-card-statements-9.7249811?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's largest bank has been fined $4.25 million by regulators for providing customers with inaccurate credit card statements.

- **[Has Canada's GDP bounced back?](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/armstrong-economy-canada-gdp-recession-9.7252719?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Economists are assessing whether Canada's economy has recovered, with GDP data offering cautious signs of resilience amid ongoing trade uncertainty.

- **[Tariff deal with Trump unlikely before U.S. midterms, says Canada's former trade chief](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/cusma-deal-u-s-midterms-9.7253072?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's former top trade negotiator warns that a resolution to ongoing U.S. tariff disputes is unlikely until after the American midterm elections, prolonging economic uncertainty.

- **[Canada is gaining corporate VCs, but still has "a participation problem," report finds](https://betakit.com/canada-is-gaining-corporate-vcs-but-still-has-a-participation-problem-report-finds/)** — *BetaKit* — A new report finds that while corporate venture capital activity is growing in Canada, overall investor participation remains insufficient to support the country's scaling startups.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News / FinLedger* — *(Evergreen relevance)* Canada's open banking journey remains a critical backdrop for the fintech sector, as the country's delayed implementation continues to shape policy and product discussions nationally.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[MX Announces Financial Intelligence Assistant to Turn Conversational AI into a Growth Engine for Financial Institutions and Their Customers](https://financialpost.com/globe-newswire/mx-announces-financial-intelligence-assistant-to-turn-conversational-ai-into-a-growth-engine-for-financial-institutions-and-their-customers)** — *Financial Post* — MX has launched an AI-powered financial assistant designed to help banks and credit unions deepen customer relationships and prevent disintermediation — directly relevant to Atlantic Canadian credit unions exploring AI adoption.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News / Bits About Money* — A deep-dive analysis of how open banking frameworks reshape payments competition, offering valuable context for Atlantic fintech stakeholders watching Canada's evolving open banking implementation.

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A critical look at the unintended consequences and consumer harms emerging from fintech products, raising important questions about financial inclusion and responsible innovation relevant to ecosystem builders in Atlantic Canada.
